summary: 10/07/2022, John Jensen deposited NPR 535,368.64 via cash transfer to Jeremy Martinez's account at PCBL (NP00000000003412850188) and simultaneously transferred the same amount to Martinez's account at SBL (NP00000000003980427782). This large cash deposit and simultaneous wire transfer to a single individual in a foreign country appears suspicious. Both banks are located in the UK.

100-200 word summary: In a Type-B Suspicious Transaction Report (STR), the compliance desk at ADBL flagged a suspicious activity involving Sally Dominguez. On 2022-10-07, she conducted a single cash withdrawal transfer of NPR 21,807.13, primarily to Henry King in the UK (GB). One of these transactions was cross-border. The amount and frequency of the transactions do not align with Dominguez's declared income profile. No adverse media or sanctions matches were found, but the rapid execution and low threshold amounts suggest potential money laundering. The STR is submitted for further analysis due to the unusual nature of the transactions.

100-200 word summary: In a periodic review, the compliance desk at KUMARI flagged suspicious transactions involving Cook, Flores and Ray (customer). On 2022-10-07, they conducted a single cross-border wire transfer of NPR 1,214,020 to Chad Mccarty, primarily through KUMARI and SBL. While the customer’s declared income profile was modest, the high aggregate value and rapid execution of the transaction, below reporting thresholds, raised concerns. No adverse media or sanctions matches were found, but the pattern of behavior aligns with Behavioral Change 2. The report was escalated due to the unusual transaction characteristics, despite the customer’s long-standing relationship with the institution. Further analysis is recommended.

10861 NPR was transferred on 2022-10-07 from Kimberly Aguirre’s account (NP00000000000156976521) at NIMB to Oconnor and Sons (counterparty) in GB via NIMB (bank). Another transfer of 10861 NPR was made on the same day from NP00000000000538393628 at PCBL to Oconnor and Sons. The transactions were cross-border and below reporting thresholds, suggesting a possible Layered Fan In pattern. The customer’s declared income profile is modest, but the velocity and aggregate value of the funds do not align with this profile. No adverse media or sanctions matches were found, but the rapid execution and low threshold amounts raised concerns. The report is submitted for further compliance analysis.

100-200 word summary: In Report ID RPT-2026-000008, suspicious activity was detected in a customer account held by Xavier Bush at CBL. On 2022-10-07, two transactions totaling NPR 2,845,747.68 via credit card occurred. The primary counterparty was C. Cooke, with additional involvement from Jennifer Kaiser. The transactions were conducted domestically and appeared to be executed in rapid succession, potentially indicative of Layered Fan Out. While no adverse media or sanctions matches were found, the transaction velocity and aggregate value did not align with the customer’s declared income profile. The account has been with the institution for several years, but the unusual timing and amount prompted an escalation to the compliance unit for further analysis. The report is submitted as an alert due to the potential risk.
